reviews.review_description: I only have the free version, but it’s the most fun zombie game I have ever played. Physics are great, in my play space I have a slight dip in the concrete and some zombies trip a bit when they cross it. I love only using the pistol and seeing how far I can go. I will buy the full version soon, I can’t wait to try this out in the multiplayer mode.

reviews.review_title: It has not worked since the last update.
reviews.review_description:
This game has been a major disappointment. We bought it specifically to play together, but we were never able to connect, and now I can't even start a game on my own.

Please make it easier for people to play together, as that is supposed to be one of the main features of the game.

In my opinion, the setup process is far too complicated. I spent hours trying different settings recommended in the game, on YouTube, and on forums, but none of them worked.


I simply want to play this game with my friend. Please provide a simple and reliable way for players to connect and play together without having to go through complicated configurations.

reviews.review_title: immediate bugs in the new update
reviews.review_description:
Hi, I was just about to play when the new update arrived. I'm glad to see this great game continuing to grow.

Unfortunately, the reason I'm writing is because the bugs started appearing almost immediately. I play with manual reload mode because I find it more challenging and fun, and the problem I had was that when I broke ammo crates, the bullets that came out stayed on the ground and weren't picked up, which really hurt my game. Another bug I noticed right away was that when I reloaded any weapon, ALL the bullets were loaded. Also, I don't know if this was actually a bug, but when I bought ammo at the store, it bought all the ammo my money allowed, not just a single pack. Another bug I noticed was purely visual with the walls; they flickered a lot, but nothing really significant.

Moving on to another point, as a personal suggestion that I think would be great for the game: Do you think it would be possible for the ammo crates you get each round to contain not only pistol ammo but also ammo for the weapons you're carrying on your belt? Or at least, could they give you bullets for the weapon you use to break the crate? The reason for this suggestion is that the limited ammo you can buy in the store REALLY falls short in later rounds. And as a final personal suggestion, I think a mechanic like giving you a second of invincibility after taking medication would be fantastic for dealing with super hordes of zombies or a bunch of bosses. especially when they corner you because of the limited space in your room (definitely my situation).

Finally, I must say that I really enjoy your game. It's games like this that make buying meta quests worthwhile. I hope you can add more content in the future, like a workshop or a Pack-a-Punch/Mystery Box that changes location between rooms each round. I wish you all the best. Cheers!

developer_response.body:
Klar, hier ist eine wärmere und noch dankbarere Version:

Hi iAndroy,

Thank you so much for taking the time to write such detailed and valuable feedback. I really appreciate it. As a solo developer, feedback like this is incredibly helpful, because it gives me a much better understanding of how players experience the game and what I should focus on improving.

I am really sorry about the bugs you ran into, and you were absolutely right to mention them. Issues like this can hurt the experience a lot, especially when playing with manual reload mode, so thank you for pointing them out so clearly.

I released a new update yesterday which should hopefully fix these problems. Objects should now be picked up properly, clips should load the correct amount of ammo into the weapon, and item purchases in the shop should also work correctly now.

I also added 3 seconds of invincibility after taking medication. I thought this was a really good idea, so thank you for suggesting it.

Regarding the ammo system, I have increased the amount of ammo you can purchase as you progress from round to round. In the long run, I think changing the flow to a different system might be the better solution, but I am not completely sure yet what the final concept should look like. I will take a closer look at this over the next few weeks.

Thank you so much again for your kind words, your support, and your thoughtful feedback. It really means a lot to me. I am definitely far from being done with the game, and I cannot wait to add more content, haha.

I have also noted your ideas about the mystery box and the workshop, and I will take a closer look at them when I find the time.

Also, if you ever have more feedback, ideas, or suggestions, please feel free to contact me anytime at ft_studio@outlook.de. I would be very happy to hear from you.

Best,
Tom

reviews.review_title: Simple Design, exciting shoot em up.
reviews.review_description: Played the demo and it felt nice and fun. For the most part the physics felt solid. The melee combat felt a little non-responsive. It was hard to tell if my hit made contact or not at times. Some improvement there would be great. Otherwise, I am looking to buying the pro version for the added guns.

reviews.review_title: Don't waste your money on pro
reviews.review_description: Worked fine during the free version. Thought I'd support the developer. However it refused to work after I paid for the pro version. Got a refund via meta support in the end. Save your money much better mixed reality games out there for half the price. Home invasion is the one I'd recommend

developer_response.body:
Hi Mattmix22,

Thank you so much for your valuable feedback. I really appreciate you taking the time to share it, and I am very sorry that you did not enjoy the game as much as I hoped.

I would love to improve the experience for everyone, so if you are open to it, I would really appreciate any more detailed feedback about what exactly happened or what you feel other games are doing better.

You can reach me anytime at ft_studio@outlook.de.

Feedback like this helps me a lot, and I would genuinely love to learn how I can make the game better for everyone.

Best,
Tom

reviews.review_description:
I've enjoyed the free version, and finally got the PRO upgrade yesterday to unlock the extra weapons. Now I love this game. Works its way up to a high energy FPS bloodfest that will keep your adrenaline going. Seeing zombies crash thru the walls of your own crib is trippy.

I really REALLY wish I could upload jpegs of my frenemies for the zombies, especially for multiplayer.

Just got a gun stock attachment and couldn't wait to try it. Game now throws an error when loading into a level. Tried clearing data like it recommended, tried recalibrating the room, and nothing. Paid the $8 and was completely worth it but now it won't let me play at all.

***EDIT***
Within 24 hours the developer reached out, and had a resolution. If anyone has an issue, definitely reach out.

developer_response.body:
Hi there,
Thanks a lot for the feedback, and I’m really sorry you’re having trouble loading the game.

Could you please send me an email at ft_studio@outlook.de ? 
I’d be happy to look into this with you and help get it working.

Best,
Tom

reviews.review_description: The reason it is mid is because you need pro for everything and all you can buy without pro is a wrench upgrades for you pistol and a grenade but the game is still fun pls fix your problems pls

developer_response.body:
Hello there,

Thank you very much for your valuable feedback. I completely understand that players would love to enjoy all modes and weapons for free.

At the same time, Spatial Zombies has been in development for many months, and as a solo developer, I would not be able to keep improving and supporting the game without the Pro unlock. It helps me cover the development costs and continue working on new updates, fixes, and improvements.

I really hope you understand, and I truly appreciate your support and feedback.

Best,
Tom

reviews.review_title: The best mixed reality zombies game.
reviews.review_description:
Edit: 
Pros-  The new update revamps the weapons and adds a couple more automatic guns and melee weapons. 
Cons - you can no longer buy more than 1 katana and once you've broken it there is no way to get another. Same goes with machetis. I suggest either making melee weapons unbreakable or back to being able to buy multiple. 
The addition of new giant type zombies though a good idea i fear they are too much of a bullet sponge and once they start throwing multiple at you it becomes a chore, sucking the fun out.
I enjoy this game and want it to get better. Looking forward to the next update.👍



Original review:
This game is great at turning your living space to your own custom zombies map. For free players the pistol is very satisfying to shoot though if you want to access more weapons you must pay for the pro version. The pro version  adds a katana, a shotgun, an assault rifle and the ability to duel wield pistols. Along with more modes to play which essentially are just different world setting like night/day and music. The night mode is more of a challenge limiting your vision and giving you a flashlight. A few occasional bugs but a few patches and hopefully an update with more weapons/zombie types will only add to the satisfying adrenaline pumping fun that is spatial zombies.

Honestly made me fall in love with vr the game is absolutely awesome, the fact you can turn any room into a zombies map and change difficulties, the free is amazing but honestly recommend buying it for the add ons very much worth it and I dont say that lightly
This is i say best workout and shooting practice all in one great game keep up good work in creating games like this like the competition highest level I got to was 21 with one pistol

Thanks to the developer. He helped me to work the game and I got to play tons of rounds. It’s real fun. and please support the developer. I think he’s working by himself in order to make this great game so hope you have fun playing a lot of zombie rounds.
￼

developer_response.body:
Hello there,

I’m really sorry that the game is not loading your space correctly. From what I’ve seen, this is usually caused by a bug with Meta’s Spatial Services, where the headset does not properly provide the space data to the game when Enhanced Spatial Services are enabled.

Please try the following steps:

1. Open the game  
2. Go to Space Setup  
3. Click the Device Permissions button  
4. Turn off Enhanced Spatial Services  
5. Restart your headset  
6. Launch the game again

Hopefully this should fix the issue 🙂

If it still does not work, please feel free to email me at ft_studio@outlook.de and I’ll gladly look into it more closely with you.

And if you do get it working, I’d really appreciate it if you could update your rating. It genuinely helps me a lot as a solo indie developer and means a ton to me.

Best,  
Tom

reviews.review_description:
Very good game！I wanted to buy the Pro version, but I found it’s no longer available.

Got it, thanks for the reply. I’d like to ask—if I unlock the Pro version, can it also be shared with another account?

developer_response.body:
Hey there!

Thanks so much for your support, it really means a lot to me 🙏

You can unlock Pro right inside the free version, and it’s exactly the same experience 😊

Due to store policies, I had to remove the standalone Pro version from the store. If a player already owns it, they can continue using it without any changes, and it will keep receiving updates. It’s just no longer available for new purchases. 

Thanks again!

Best,
Tom

reviews.review_description: First time in my life, I have ever written a review for product until now. I can’t wait to see the final product. Great job creators ￼

I have to say that by far this is the game I go to in VR. It's not perfect, needs a lot of love but it's getting there. Like any complaint I may have is simply coz this is still being developed and it has gotten upgrades , big ones in the last little bit. So it's getting there!

Worth the time and money. Just needs a CO-OP mode please!! :)

developer_response.body:
Hi there,

Thanks a lot for supporting the game!

Yes, the game does have a multi room feature. Please enable it in the game settings and configure each room you want to play in using Space Setup.

Once that is done, you can freely walk between the rooms and fight zombies in every room.

If you still run into any issues, feel free to contact me anytime at forstech@outlook.de.

Best,
Tom

reviews.review_description: Me and my dad have the quest 3s and the multiplayer function doesn’t work at all it says 0 players connected but otherwise it’s really fun but please try to fix it

developer_response.body:
Hi there,

Thanks so much for your support, and I’m sorry you’ve been running into multiplayer pairing issues.
I’ve just released a new version with improved multiplayer. 🙂

After updating, you’ll see two connection options:

- Auto mode (recommended): This works the same as before but includes several bug fixes, so it should be more reliable now. I’d love to hear how it performs for you, this is the preferred way to connect.

- Manual mode: In this mode, both players place two markers at the same positions on the ground. These markers are then used to align and synchronize the play space between devices.

Please let me know how it goes!

Best,
Tom

reviews.review_description:
This game has potential, but I wouldn't buy it as is right now. If the pro version only unlocks some modes and weapons, then my 2 biggest problems aren't solved.

1st, I scanned in my hallway that's attached to the living room, and of course, it has doors.  SOOO if you're in the living room, the zombies can spawn out of those doors and just walk through the hallway wall and kill you. So, the game recognizes there's a solid wall there, but the most basic zombies can just walk through them?

2nd, I don't want to talk a bunch of crap about the dev, I know this is a fantasy game, and even with the zombies and autoreloading firearms suspending disbelief, my biggest problem is that fudging grenade. I'm aware the average human hasn't thrown a grenade before, but that fragmentation grenade CANNOT go off in your hand just because you pulled the pin. That's not how grenades work. I'd like to support the dev and purchase the pro version, but until that frag grenade is fixed, it's a thumbs down from me.

The game is amazing like you get to make your own space and all sorts of crazy stuff it's amazing I love it you should get it Even know that a while the weapons are on pro it's still good it's a Nice little game that you can have
I have the free version and wanted to get the pro version, is there any way to still get it?
I bought the paid version of the game. The game has been removed from the shelves and can't be played online. Can't you explain why?
I played my son's VR Game once and bought my own the same night. I play Spacial zombies everyday!!  I love it!!
The game is so fun and cool

Started playing this again, completely different.  Clearly the devs have never actually handled a rifle or shotgun before,  because they DON'T rotate off your shoulder. Impossible to aim with them in the game, makes no sense that it's easier to use with one hand than two.

the game has added so much more features that you can't get without buying the continuation of the game which i think is now worth it this game is really cool now and worth the continuation and i think it deserves five stars now
Great game, but the unlocked Pro version can’t be shared with sub-accounts, so it means we have to buy two copies.

Haven’t had this much fun on VR since my first VR experience playing beat saber and super-hot. At 8$ its a great deal. I’ve avoided many VR games because i get bad motion sickness right around 30 minutes. The mixed reality eliminates this. Ive played this game multiple times til my headset hits 0% battery. what guy doesn’t wanna get home from work and protect his house from zombies. 

GAME HAS POTENTIAL TO BE A $30-$40 TITLE. The game is very basic right now but needs COOP/COLOCATION. 2-4 PLAYERS. MORE WEAPONS that take similar ammo, SMG, auto shotgun, some variety in rifles. Then a cash reward system and THROWABLE ITEMS, noise makers, Molotov’s, grenades rebuildable barricades, and maybe a mystery box. Can anyone say Nacht der untoten? Ive sent clips of this game to my friends and 2 of them were about to go buy a VR and come over to my house for the weekend, because they assumed colocation would already be a thing. 
DEVS YOU ARE SITTING ON A GOLD MINE! If YOU DONT DO IT FIRST SOMEONE ELSE WILL! And yes i am yelling. Me and all the other middle aged boys just want a a way to relive COD zombies IRL in VR.

UPDATE: just got to play the new version just in time for Christmas, money system is awesome also the store menu gives you a minute to refresh or “pause” in between levels. The katana and grenades are awesome!!! Nice touch with the body’s falling apart. It was awesome to see a zombies get skyrocketed into pieces through my ceiling when he was standing on a grenade. Keep up the good work! 
I do miss putting both pistols high on my torso but that isn’t too big of a deal, also i like how you limited the ammo per round and the game seems more challenging now which is awesome.

developer_response.body:
Hi there! 
Thanks so much for your review, I really appreciate the thoughtful feedback!

I took your suggestions to heart and worked on the points you mentioned.
I’m happy to share that local co-op multiplayer is now finally in the game, and I’d love to hear what you think about it! 

Over the past few months, I’ve also added a variety of new weapons and introduced some boss enemies to make the experience more exciting.

I hope you and your friends have a great time playing! 
There are also more updates on the way, so stay tuned!

Best,
Tom

reviews.review_title: My stress level is delightfully high
reviews.review_description: This is my first review of a game ever, but I love this game. Within an hour of play I purchased the full version. I am sweating by level 8, and my heart is racing at any level higher than that. I have been “surprise attacked” by Zombies that came out of a wall behind me, but I may have my volume down to low to hear the cracking. I texted a buddy, and after a day, he began moving furniture around in his house so that he can have a bigger play area. Thank you for such a fun and entertaining game.

Very nice game zombies are scary. Wish the were more weapons and a coop mode

So I’ve been playing the game for 5 days now, and overall I think it’s great fun.

Zombies – There’s a nice mix of different zombies. Some come at you slowly, while others are fast, which I really like. I’d say there are around five different zombie designs.

Weapons – There’s a great choice of weapons, from pistols to the katana samurai sword, which are both my favourites. The guns have great accuracy and feel true to aim. I think some work could be done to make the katana feel more natural, as sometimes the first swing doesn’t seem to register, but overall it’s still solid.

Mixed Reality – The mixed-reality mode is great; I’ve had so much fun playing in my living room. I’d love the option to have just doors and windows, with an optional “break walls” feature you can turn on if you want.

Virtual Reality – Very cool as well! It would be great to have a few more room designs.

Controls – Overall, the controls are easy to pick up, and honestly, I don’t think they need much improvement.

I’ve only been playing for 5 days, so I’m not sure how the game was before or how much it’s been improved, but like I said, I’ve had a blast so far and made some cool content. I hope they continue to update the game because it has a lot of potential. Well done, guys 👏
Greatest game in is the best game i have and now i have pistol in life
